ex.en: I totally agree with you.

tourism.en: the business of providing travel and activities for tourists

ex.en: Tourism is a major industry in this city.

tournament.en: a competition in which players compete in a series of games until one winner remains

ex.en: She won the tennis tournament last weekend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난 주말 테니스 대회에서 우승했다.

trace.en: find or discover the origin or cause of something by careful investigation

ex.en: Scientists traced the disease back to a virus.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 병의 원인을 바이러스까지 추적해 밝혀냈다.

trace.en: a visible mark or sign that shows something existed or happened

ex.en: There was no trace of the missing wallet.

trace.en: a very small amount of something

ex.en: There was a trace of sadness in her voice.
ex.ko: 그녀의 목소리에는 약간의 슬픔이 묻어 있었다.

tracksuit.en: a loose, comfortable set of clothes worn for exercise or sports

ex.en: He wore a tracksuit for his morning run.
ex.ko: 그는 아침 조깅을 위해 운동복을 입었다.

traditionally.en: according to tradition or customs handed down over time

ex.en: Traditionally, the family gathers for dinner on Sundays.
ex.ko: 전통적으로 그 가족은 일요일마다 저녁 식사를 위해 모인다.

traffic jam.en: a long line of vehicles that cannot move or move very slowly

ex.en: We were stuck in a traffic jam for an hour.
ex.ko: 우리는 한 시간 동안 교통 체증에 갇혀 있었다.

tragedy.en: a very sad event that causes great suffering or loss

ex.en: The fire was a terrible tragedy for the whole town.
ex.ko: 그 화재는 마을 전체에 끔찍한 비극이었다.

tragedy.en: a serious play that ends unhappily for the main character

ex.en: Shakespeare wrote many famous tragedies.
ex.ko: 셰익스피어는 유명한 비극을 여러 편 썼다.

tragic.en: very sad, especially because it involves death or suffering

ex.en: Her death was a tragic accident.

trail.en: be behind someone or something in a race, competition, or contest

ex.en: The team was trailing by ten points at halftime.

trail.en: a rough path through the countryside, mountains, or woods

ex.en: We hiked along a mountain trail all day.
ex.ko: 우리는 하루 종일 산길을 따라 하이킹했다.

trail.en: a line of marks or signs left behind by something that has passed

ex.en: The plane left a white trail across the sky.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 하늘에 하얀 자국을 남겼다.

transfer.en: move something or someone from one place to another

ex.en: She transferred the files to a new computer.

transfer.en: a change from one bus, train, or plane to another during a journey

ex.en: You need a transfer to get to the airport.

transfer.en: move to a different job, school, or place within an organization

ex.en: He transferred to the Seoul office last year.

transform.en: change completely in form, appearance, or nature

ex.en: The old warehouse was transformed into a modern gallery.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 창고는 현대적인 갤러리로 완전히 바뀌었다.

transformation.en: a complete change in form or appearance

ex.en: The city has undergone a huge transformation in ten years.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 10년 만에 큰 변화를 겪었다.

transitional.en: relating to a period of change from one state or stage to another

ex.en: University is a transitional period between school and work.

translate.en: change words from one language into another

ex.en: Can you translate this letter into English?
ex.ko: 이 편지를 영어로 번역해 줄 수 있어?

translate.en: result in something or cause a particular effect

ex.en: Higher sales didn't translate into bigger profits.
ex.ko: 매출 증가가 더 큰 이익으로 이어지지는 않았다.

transport.en: carry people or goods from one place to another

ex.en: The goods are transported by truck.

transport.en: a means or system of carrying people or goods from place to place

ex.en: Public transport is cheap and convenient here.

transportation.en: a system or means of moving people or goods from place to place

ex.en: The city has an excellent public transportation system.
ex.ko: 이 도시는 훌륭한 대중교통 체계를 갖추고 있다.

trash.en: waste material that is thrown away

ex.en: Please take out the trash before you leave.
ex.ko: 나가기 전에 쓰레기 좀 내다 버려 줘.

ex.en: Someone trashed the hotel room during the party.
ex.ko: 누군가 파티 도중 호텔 방을 엉망으로 만들어 놓았다.

travel agent.en: a person who arranges trips and tours for customers

ex.en: The travel agent booked our flights and hotel.
ex.ko: 여행사 직원이 우리 항공편과 호텔을 예약해 주었다.

treat.en: behave toward someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: She treats everyone with respect.

treat.en: give medical care to someone who is ill or injured

ex.en: The doctor treated her broken arm.

treat.en: something special given or done to give someone pleasure

ex.en: Ice cream is a nice treat on a hot day.
ex.ko: 아이스크림은 더운 날 기분 좋은 선물 같은 거야.

treatment.en: medical care given to cure an illness or injury

ex.en: She is receiving treatment for her illness.

treatment.en: the way someone behaves toward or deals with a person

ex.en: The workers demanded fair treatment.

tremble.en: shake slightly, especially because of fear, cold, or weakness

ex.en: Her hands trembled with fear.

tremendous.en: very great in amount, size, or degree

ex.en: She made a tremendous effort to finish on time.
ex.ko: 그녀는 제때 끝내려고 엄청난 노력을 기울였다.

trend.en: a general direction in which something is changing or developing

ex.en: There is a trend toward remote work.

trend.en: a fashion or style that is popular at a particular time

ex.en: Short hair is a big trend this year.

trigger.en: cause something to start or happen suddenly

ex.en: The news triggered a wave of protests.

trigger.en: the small lever you pull to fire a gun

ex.en: He pulled the trigger without hesitation.

trim.en: cut a small amount off something to make it neater or the right size

ex.en: He trimmed the hedge in front of his house.

trim.en: reduce the amount or size of something

ex.en: The company trimmed its budget this year.

triumph.en: a great victory or success, especially after a difficult struggle

ex.en: Winning the championship was a real triumph for the team.
ex.ko: 우승은 그 팀에게 진짜 큰 승리였다.

ex.en: She triumphed over many hardships to become a doctor.
ex.ko: 그녀는 많은 역경을 이겨내고 의사가 되었다.

tropical.en: relating to or typical of the hot, humid regions near the equator

ex.en: We visited a tropical island last summer.

trumpet.en: a brass musical instrument with a bright, loud sound

ex.en: He plays the trumpet in a jazz band.

trustworthy.en: able to be trusted or relied on

ex.en: She is a trustworthy friend who always keeps her promises.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 약속을 지키는 신뢰할 수 있는 친구다.

tumble.en: fall suddenly, especially by losing your balance or footing

ex.en: The little boy tumbled down the stairs.

tumble.en: decrease suddenly and by a large amount

ex.en: Stock prices tumbled after the announcement.

tuna.en: a large sea fish often eaten as food, including in cans

ex.en: She made a tuna sandwich for lunch.

turbulence.en: sudden, violent movements of air that shake an aircraft

ex.en: The plane shook because of turbulence.

turbulence.en: a state of confusion, disorder, or sudden change

ex.en: The country went through years of political turbulence.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 수년간의 정치적 혼란을 겪었다.

turning.en: a place where a road turns, or a change in direction

ex.en: Take the next turning on the left.

turtle.en: a reptile with a hard shell that lives in water or on land

ex.en: We saw a turtle swimming near the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변 근처에서 헤엄치는 거북을 봤다.

twin.en: one of two children born to the same mother at the same time

ex.en: My sister and I are twins.

twin.en: being one of a pair that are exactly the same

ex.en: The hotel room had twin beds.
ex.ko: 그 호텔 방에는 트윈 침대(같은 두 개의 침대)가 있었다.

twist.en: turn or bend something with force, often into a spiral shape

ex.en: She twisted the wire into a small circle.
ex.ko: 그녀는 철사를 비틀어 작은 원 모양으로 만들었다.

twist.en: injure a joint such as your ankle by turning it suddenly and awkwardly

ex.en: He twisted his ankle while running.

twist.en: a sudden, unexpected change in a story or situation

ex.en: The movie has a surprising twist at the end.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 마지막에 놀라운 반전이 있다.

typical.en: having the usual qualities or features of a particular type of thing

ex.en: It was a typical rainy day in London.

typically.en: in a way that is usual or expected

ex.en: Meetings typically last about an hour.
ex.ko: 회의는 일반적으로 한 시간 정도 걸린다.

unable.en: not having the ability, means, or opportunity to do something

ex.en: I was unable to finish the report on time.
ex.ko: 나는 제때 보고서를 끝낼 수 없었다.

ex.en: She was unafraid to speak her mind.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자기 생각을 말하는 것을 두려워하지 않았다.

unbelievable.en: so surprising or extreme that it is hard to believe

ex.en: The view from the top was unbelievable.
ex.ko: 정상에서 본 경치는 믿을 수 없을 정도였다.

uncertainty.en: a situation in which something is not known or certain

ex.en: There is a lot of uncertainty about the future of the company.

unclear.en: not easy to understand or not clearly explained

ex.en: The instructions were unclear, so we got lost.
He burst into uncontrollable laughter.
ex.ko: 그는 걷잡을 수 없이 웃음을 터뜨렸다.

uncover.en: discover or reveal information that was secret or hidden

ex.en: Reporters uncovered evidence of the scandal.

underage.en: younger than the legal age required for something

ex.en: It is illegal to sell alcohol to underage drinkers.

undergo.en: experience or go through something, especially a change or difficulty

ex.en: She had to undergo surgery last month.
The cat was hiding underneath the bed.

underpants.en: a piece of underwear worn on the lower part of the body

ex.en: He packed clean underpants for the trip.
ex.ko: 그는 여행을 위해 깨끗한 속옷을 챙겼다.

undo.en: reverse the effect of an action to return to how things were before

ex.en: Press Ctrl+Z to undo your last change.
She undid the top button of her shirt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 셔츠 맨 위 단추를 풀었다.

undress.en: take off your clothes or someone else's clothes

ex.en: The child undressed quickly and got into the bath.
ex.ko: 그 아이는 재빨리 옷을 벗고 욕조에 들어갔다.

ex.en: He has been unemployed since the factory closed.
ex.ko: 그는 공장이 문을 닫은 뒤로 무직 상태다.

unemployment.en: the state of not having a job

ex.en: Unemployment rose sharply during the recession.
ex.ko: 경기 침체 동안 실업률이 급격히 올랐다.

ex.en: We had an unexpected visitor last night.

unexpectedly.en: in a way that was not expected

ex.en: Her grandmother arrived unexpectedly.

unfairly.en: in a way that is not fair or reasonable

ex.en: He felt he had been treated unfairly at work.

unfit.en: not suitable or good enough for a particular purpose

ex.en: The old bridge was declared unfit for use.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 다리는 사용하기에 부적합하다고 판정되었다.

ex.en: He felt unfit after months without exercise.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 달 동안 운동을 안 해서 체력이 부족하다고 느꼈다.

unfold.en: open out something that was folded

ex.en: She unfolded the map on the table.

unfold.en: gradually develop or become known over time

ex.en: We watched the story unfold on the news.
ex.ko: 우리는 뉴스에서 그 이야기가 전개되는 것을 지켜봤다.

unfortunate.en: having bad luck, or causing regret

ex.en: It was unfortunate that she missed the flight.
ex.ko: 그녀가 비행기를 놓친 것은 안타까운 일이었다.

unfriendly.en: not friendly or welcoming toward other people

ex.en: The waiter was rude and unfriendly.

unheard.en: not heard, noticed, or paid attention to

ex.en: Her complaints went unheard.

unify.en: bring separate parts together to form a single whole

ex.en: The new leader tried to unify the divided country.
ex.ko: 새 지도자는 분열된 나라를 통합하려 했다.

ex.en: He seemed uninterested in the conversation.
ex.ko: 그는 그 대화에 관심이 없어 보였다.

ex.en: The lecture was long and uninteresting.

union.en: an organization formed by workers to protect their rights and interests

ex.en: The workers joined a union to negotiate better pay.
ex.ko: 근로자들은 더 나은 임금을 협상하기 위해 노동조합에 가입했다.

union.en: a group of countries or organizations joined together for a common purpose

ex.en: The European Union has many member countries.

unique.en: being the only one of its kind; very special or unusual

ex.en: Every snowflake is unique.

unite.en: join together for a common purpose or to form a single group

ex.en: The two parties united to form a new government.
ex.ko: 두 정당은 새 정부를 구성하기 위해 통합했다.

universe.en: all of space, including every star, planet, and galaxy that exists

ex.en: Scientists study how the universe began.

unless.en: except if; used to say what will happen if something does not happen

ex.en: We won't leave unless it stops raining.
ex.ko: 비가 그치지 않으면 우리는 출발하지 않을 것이다.

unlikely.en: not likely to happen or be true

ex.en: It's unlikely to rain today.
We were unlucky with the weather on our trip.
ex.ko: 우리는 여행 중 날씨 운이 없었다.

unpack.en: take things out of a bag, box, or suitcase

ex.en: She unpacked her suitcase as soon as she got home.

unpredictable.cue: 어떻게 될지 미리 알 수 없는
unpredictable.en: impossible to know or guess in advance

ex.en: The weather here is completely unpredictable.
ex.ko: 이곳의 날씨는 전혀 예측할 수 없다.

unrelated.en: not connected or having no relationship with something

ex.en: The two events are completely unrelated.
ex.ko: 그 두 사건은 서로 완전히 관련이 없다.

ex.en: His room is always untidy.

unusually.en: to a degree that is not usual or normal

ex.en: It was unusually cold this morning.
She threw away the unwanted clothes.
He felt unwell and went home early.
ex.ko: 그는 몸이 안 좋아서 일찍 집에 갔다.

up-to-date.en: reflecting the most recent information or trends

ex.en: Make sure your software is up-to-date.

update.en: add new information or make something more modern and current

ex.en: Please update your contact details.

update.en: the newest information about a situation

ex.en: He gave us an update on the project.
ex.ko: 그는 우리에게 그 프로젝트의 최신 정보를 알려주었다.

uplifted.en: feeling happier or more hopeful than before

ex.en: I felt uplifted after hearing her speech.
ex.ko: 그녀의 연설을 듣고 나서 나는 기분이 고양되었다.

upload.en: send data or a file from your device to the internet or a server

ex.en: She uploaded the photos to the website.

upper.en: higher in position than something else

ex.en: She lives on the upper floor of the building.

upward.en: moving or directed toward a higher place or level

ex.en: There has been an upward trend in prices.
The balloon floated upward into the sky.
ex.ko: 그 풍선은 하늘로 위쪽을 향해 떠올랐다.

urge.en: strongly encourage or push someone to do something

ex.en: Doctors urged him to quit smoking.

urge.en: a strong desire to do something

ex.en: She felt an urge to cry.
This is an urgent matter that needs your attention.
ex.ko: 이것은 당신의 주의가 필요한 긴급한 사안이다.

ex.en: Water usage increases in summer.

usage.en: the way words or language are normally used

ex.en: The dictionary explains the usage of each word.
ex.ko: 그 사전은 각 단어의 어법을 설명해 준다.

used to.en: used to describe something that happened regularly in the past but does not happen now

ex.en: I used to play the piano when I was young.
ex.ko: 나는 어릴 때 피아노를 치곤 했다.

useless.en: not useful or effective for any purpose

ex.en: This old phone is useless now.

vacancy.en: a job or position that is available and not yet filled

ex.en: There is a vacancy for a sales manager.

vacancy.en: an empty room available to rent, especially in a hotel

ex.en: The hotel had no vacancies for the weekend.
He gave a vague answer to the question.

vain.en: too proud of your own appearance or achievements

ex.en: He is too vain to admit his mistakes.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 허영심이 많아서 자기 실수를 인정하지 못한다.

ex.en: They made a vain attempt to save the building.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 건물을 구하려는 헛된 시도를 했다.

valuable.en: worth a lot of money or very useful and important

ex.en: This is a valuable piece of advice.

van.en: a vehicle used to carry goods or a group of people

ex.en: The delivery van parked outside the store.
ex.ko: 배달용 밴이 가게 밖에 주차되어 있었다.

variety.en: the quality of including many different types of things

ex.en: The menu offers a lot of variety.

variety.en: a number of different types of the same thing

ex.en: The store sells a variety of fruits.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 여러 가지 과일을 판다.

ex.en: The shop sells various kinds of tea.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 다양한 종류의 차를 판다.

vary.en: be different or change according to circumstances

ex.en: Prices vary depending on the season.

vast.en: extremely large in size, amount, or extent

ex.en: The desert covers a vast area.
ex.ko: 그 사막은 광대한 지역을 뒤덮고 있다.

vegetarian.en: a person who does not eat meat or fish

ex.en: She has been a vegetarian for ten years.

vegetarian.en: consisting of food that does not contain meat or fish

ex.en: The restaurant has a vegetarian menu.

vehicle.en: a machine, especially with wheels or engines, that carries people or goods

ex.en: Parking is limited to one vehicle per household.
ex.ko: 주차는 가구당 한 대의 차량으로 제한된다.

vein.en: one of the tubes that carry blood from the body back to the heart

ex.en: The nurse found a vein to draw blood.

vessel.en: a tube in the body through which blood flows

ex.en: A blood vessel burst in his eye.
The vessel sailed into the harbor at dawn.

vet.en: a doctor who treats sick or injured animals

ex.en: We took the dog to the vet this morning.
ex.ko: 우리는 오늘 아침 개를 수의사에게 데려갔다.

vet.en: carefully check the suitability or background of someone or something

ex.en: The company vets all job applicants carefully.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 모든 지원자를 꼼꼼히 검증한다.

via.en: traveling through a place on the way to a destination, or by means of

ex.en: We flew to Paris via London.

vice.en: an immoral or bad habit or behavior

ex.en: Smoking is his only vice.

vice president.en: an official ranking just below the president of a country or company

ex.en: She was elected vice president of the company.

victim.en: a person who is harmed or killed by a crime, accident, or disease

ex.en: The victims of the accident were taken to the hospital.

victory.en: success in a battle, game, or competition

ex.en: The team celebrated their victory with the fans.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 팬들과 함께 승리를 축하했다.

video clip.en: a short piece of video, often shared online

ex.en: She shared a funny video clip with her friends.
ex.ko: 그녀는 친구들에게 재미있는 동영상 클립을 공유했다.

viewpoint.en: a way of thinking about or looking at something

ex.en: From my viewpoint, the plan seems too risky.
ex.ko: 내 관점에서는 그 계획이 너무 위험해 보인다.

violence.en: physical force used to hurt or damage someone or something

ex.en: The movie contains scenes of violence.

violently.en: in a violent or forceful manner

ex.en: The ship shook violently in the storm.
ex.ko: 그 배는 폭풍 속에서 격렬하게 흔들렸다.

virtual.en: existing or created by computer technology rather than physically

ex.en: The students attended a virtual meeting online.

virus.en: a very small living thing that can cause disease

ex.en: The flu is caused by a virus.

virus.en: a program that can damage files and programs on a computer

ex.en: Her laptop got infected with a virus.

visa.en: an official mark in a passport that allows you to enter a country

ex.en: You need a visa to visit that country.
The mountain was clearly visible from the window.
Her vision has gotten worse with age.

vision.en: an idea or picture of what something could be like in the future

ex.en: The leader shared her vision for the company.
ex.ko: 그 리더는 회사의 미래에 대한 비전을 공유했다.

ex.en: The presentation had strong visual effects.

visually.en: in a way that relates to sight

ex.en: The app is visually easy to use.

vivid.en: producing clear, sharp, and lifelike images in the mind

ex.en: She has a vivid memory of her childhood home.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어린 시절 집에 대한 생생한 기억을 가지고 있다.

vividly.en: in a vivid and lifelike way

ex.en: I still remember the accident vividly.
ex.ko: 나는 아직도 그 사고를 생생하게 기억한다.

volcano.en: a mountain with an opening through which hot lava and gases erupt

ex.en: The volcano erupted after years of silence.
ex.ko: 그 화산은 수년의 침묵 끝에 폭발했다.

volume.en: the loudness or softness of a sound

ex.en: Please turn down the volume on the TV.

volume.en: the amount of space that something takes up, or the total amount of something

ex.en: The volume of traffic increases during rush hour.

volunteer.en: a person who does work without being paid

ex.en: Volunteers helped clean up the beach.

volunteer.en: offer to do something without being asked or paid

ex.en: He volunteered to help with the project.

vowel.cue: a, e, i, o, u 같은 소리·글자
vowel.en: a speech sound made without blocking air, or a letter representing it (a, e, i, o, u)

ex.en: English has five main vowel letters.
ex.ko: 영어에는 다섯 개의 주요 모음 글자가 있다.

voyage.en: a long journey, especially by sea or in space

ex.en: The ship's voyage across the ocean took two weeks.

wagon.en: a vehicle with wheels, usually pulled by an animal, used for carrying loads

ex.en: The farmer loaded hay onto the wagon.

wagon.en: a car with a long body and extra space at the back for luggage

ex.en: They bought a station wagon for family trips.
ex.ko: 그들은 가족 여행을 위해 왜건 차량을 샀다.

ward.en: a section of a hospital where patients with similar needs are cared for

ex.en: She works in the children's ward at the hospital.

ward.en: a young person who is legally under the protection of another person or the state

ex.en: The child became a ward of the state after her parents died.
ex.ko: 그 아이는 부모가 세상을 떠난 뒤 국가의 피후견인이 되었다.

wardrobe.en: a tall piece of furniture used for storing clothes

ex.en: She hung her coat in the wardrobe.

wardrobe.en: all the clothes that a person owns

ex.en: She bought new clothes to update her summer wardrobe.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여름 옷을 업데이트하려고 새 옷을 샀다.

warmly.en: in a friendly, kind, or affectionate way

ex.en: She welcomed us warmly at the door.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문 앞에서 우리를 따뜻하게 맞이했다.

warmth.en: a pleasant, moderate amount of heat

ex.en: We enjoyed the warmth of the fire.

warmth.en: kindness and affection shown toward other people

ex.en: Her warmth made everyone feel welcome.

warn.en: tell someone about a possible danger or problem in advance

ex.en: The sign warns drivers about the sharp curve ahead.
ex.ko: 그 표지판은 앞에 급커브가 있다고 운전자들에게 경고한다.

warning.en: a statement or sign that tells you about a possible danger or problem

ex.en: The weather service issued a storm warning.

warranty.cue: 제품 고장 시 무상 수리를 약속하는
warranty.en: a written promise that a product will be repaired or replaced if it breaks within a certain time

ex.en: The laptop comes with a two-year warranty.

warrior.en: a person who fights in battles, especially a brave or experienced one

ex.en: The ancient warriors were known for their courage.

washbowl.en: a bathroom sink used for washing your hands and face

ex.en: He washed his face at the washbowl.

waste.en: material that is thrown away because it is no longer needed

ex.en: The factory produces a lot of waste.

waste.en: the careless or unnecessary use of something valuable, such as time or money

ex.en: Watching TV all day is a waste of time.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 TV를 보는 것은 시간 낭비다.

waste.en: use something valuable carelessly or for no good reason

ex.en: Don't waste your money on things you don't need.
ex.ko: 필요 없는 것에 돈을 낭비하지 마.

wasteful.en: using more of something, such as time or money, than is necessary

ex.en: It's wasteful to leave the lights on all day.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 불을 켜 두는 것은 낭비적이다.

waterfall.en: a place where a river or stream falls steeply from a height

ex.en: We hiked to see a beautiful waterfall.
ex.ko: 우리는 아름다운 폭포를 보러 하이킹을 갔다.

wax.en: a soft substance that melts easily, used to make candles or polish things

ex.en: The candle was made of beeswax.

wax.en: put a layer of wax on something to make it shine or protect it

ex.en: He waxed his car every weekend.
ex.ko: 그는 주말마다 자기 차에 왁스를 발랐다.

weakness.en: a fault or area where someone or something is not strong

ex.en: Public speaking is his biggest weakness.
ex.ko: 대중 앞에서 말하는 것이 그의 가장 큰 약점이다.

weapon.en: an object used to hurt or kill someone or to fight with

ex.en: The soldiers carried their weapons carefully.

weather forecast.en: a prediction of what the weather will be like

ex.en: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 일기예보에 따르면 내일 비가 온다고 한다.

webcam.en: a small camera connected to a computer, used to send video over the internet

ex.en: She used her webcam for the video call.
ex.ko: 그녀는 화상 통화를 위해 웹캠을 사용했다.

weird.en: strange or unusual in a way that seems surprising or hard to explain

ex.en: It was weird that he didn't answer his phone.
ex.ko: 그가 전화를 안 받은 것이 이상했다.

well-dressed.en: wearing neat, attractive, or fashionable clothes

ex.en: A well-dressed man walked into the office.
ex.ko: 옷을 잘 차려입은 남자가 사무실로 걸어 들어왔다.

ex.en: The ship sailed westward across the Pacific.
ex.ko: 그 배는 태평양을 가로질러 서쪽으로 항해했다.

wetland.en: an area of low land that is often covered with water

ex.en: Many birds live in the wetland near the river.
ex.ko: 강 근처 습지에는 많은 새들이 산다.

whale.en: a very large sea mammal that breathes air through a hole on its head

ex.en: We saw a whale swimming near the boat.
ex.ko: 우리는 배 근처에서 헤엄치는 고래를 보았다.
